senses.definition: a form of rugby where teams of 15 players each compete to score points by carrying, passing, and kicking an oval-shaped ball to score tries or kick goals

examples.x: We played a thrilling game of rugby union yesterday.
He excels at tackling in rugby union.
She scored a try in her first rugby union match.
The rugby union team is practicing hard for the upcoming tournament.
He watches rugby union matches every weekend.

senses.definition: a form of rugby football played with teams of 13 players, featuring more structured gameplay and frequent ball-handling

examples.x: He scored a try in yesterday's rugby league game.
He plays rugby league for his local team.
Rugby league is known for its fast-paced action.
She's a fan of both rugby union and rugby league.
The rugby league team won the championship last year.

senses.definition: a team sport played by individuals with disabilities, combining elements of rugby, basketball, and handball

examples.x: Wheelchair rugby is an intense and fast-paced sport.
He trains daily to improve his wheelchair rugby skills.
Wheelchair rugby players need specially designed wheelchairs.
He was introduced to wheelchair rugby after his accident.
The local community supports wheelchair rugby events.

senses.definition: (rugby) a catch made from a kick within a player's own 22-meter area, allowing them to call for a free kick

examples.x: He called a mark to stop the play.
She made a mark and took the free kick.
The player signaled a mark after catching the ball.
He caught the ball and claimed a mark.
The mark gave them a chance to regroup.

senses.definition: (rugby) a kick at goal awarded after a try, worth two points if successful

examples.x: He scored the conversion to add two points.
They celebrated after he nailed the conversion.
The conversion was successful, boosting their lead.
The conversion attempt hit the post and bounced out.
The team's kicker lined up for the conversion.

senses.definition: an illegal pass in rugby that moves the ball toward the opponent's goal line, leading to a scrum for the other team

examples.x: The referee called a throw forward.
He was penalized for a throw forward.
The team lost possession due to a throw forward.
A throw forward stopped their attacking play.
She mistakenly made a throw forward under pressure.

senses.definition: a low kick that makes the ball bounce and roll unpredictably along the ground in rugby

examples.x: He used a grubber kick to get past the defenders.
The grubber kick bounced perfectly for the winger.
She practiced her grubber kicks to improve accuracy.
He surprised everyone with a well-placed grubber kick.
The coach praised his effective grubber kick strategy.

senses.definition: a foul in rugby where a player accidentally knocks the ball forward with their hand or arm, resulting in a stoppage of play and a scrum awarded to the opposing team

examples.x: He lost possession after a knock-on near the try line.
The team conceded possession due to a knock-on in midfiel
The winger's knock-on led to a turnover in possession.
A knock-on is considered a handling error in rugby.

senses.definition: the period of active gameplay in rugby when the ball is in motion and not in a set piece, such as a scrum or lineout

examples.x: The team excels in open play with their fast-paced style.
In open play, players must be quick to react to changes on the field.
He thrives in open play, using his speed to outrun opponents.
She made a decisive break in open play, leading to a try.
Mistakes in open play can quickly turn the tide of a game.

senses.definition: (rugby) the conclusion of a match with no winner declared, usually due to unforeseen circumstances like weather or injury

examples.x: The rugby match ended in a no side due to a severe thunderstorm.
They had to call a no side because of a player's serious injury.
Due to a scheduling conflict, the teams agreed to a no side.
The game ended in a no side due to a dispute between the teams.
No side was called when the medical team couldn't reach the injured player in time.

senses.definition: the line at each end of the rugby field that a player must cross with the ball to score a try

examples.x: He sprinted toward the try line to score.
He crossed the try line with a powerful run.
She dived over the try line for the winning try.
The opposing team's defense blocked every attempt to breach the try line.
Scoring a try just short of the try line was frustrating for the team.

senses.definition: the boundary line at each end of the rugby field where the ball is considered out of play

examples.x: The player kicked the ball over the dead-ball line.
The referee signaled a lineout after the ball crossed the dead-ball line.
He tackled the opponent just short of the dead-ball line.
The ball bounced into touch behind the dead-ball line.
The kick went out on the full, crossing the dead-ball line directly.

senses.definition: a method of restarting play in soccer and similar sports where a player throws the ball back into play from the sideline

examples.x: The referee awarded a throw-in to the opposing team.
He took a quick throw-in to catch the defense off guard.
She practiced her throw-in technique to improve distance and accuracy.
The team lost possession after an illegal throw-in.
The referee signaled for a throw-in after the ball went out of bounds.

senses.definition: a method of restarting play after the ball goes out of bounds, where rugby players from each team compete for the ball thrown in from the sideline

examples.x: Lineouts are crucial moments in rugby matches.
The team practiced their lineout strategies tirelessly.
Their lineout dominance propelled them to victory.
A clean catch in the lineout set up their attacking play.
The opposition stole possession in the lineout.

senses.definition: a method of scoring where a player kicks the ball through the goalposts during open play

examples.x: He scored a drop goal to break the tie.
The drop goal secured the victory for their team.
He practiced his drop goal technique tirelessly.
They celebrated wildly after the successful drop goal.
The opposing team was caught off guard by the drop goal.

senses.definition: (rugby) a score achieved by placing the ball on or beyond the opponent's goal line

examples.x: He scored a touchdown after a powerful run.
She celebrated wildly after her team's touchdown.
She made a diving catch for the game-winning touchdown.
He crossed the goal line for an easy touchdown.
The team's strategy paid off with a well-executed touchdown.

senses.definition: a method of restarting the play by a rugby player tapping the ball with their hand instead of kicking it after a penalty is awarded

examples.x: She quickly took a tap penalty to keep the momentum going.
The referee signaled for a tap penalty near the try line.
They practiced their tap penalty moves during training.
He took a tap penalty and passed to a teammate.
The tap penalty caught the opposing team by surprise.

senses.definition: a situation where rugby players from both teams bind together around a ball carrier who is on their feet

examples.x: The team formed a maul to push forward.
He joined the maul to support his teammate.
The maul collapsed, resulting in a turnover.
She secured the ball in the maul.
Our team executed a powerful maul near the opponent's try line.

senses.definition: the phase of play where rugby players from both teams contest possession of the ball on the ground, formed after a tackle

examples.x: The players quickly formed a ruck after the tackle.
He dove into the ruck to secure the ball.
The team worked together to drive the ruck forward.
He joined the ruck to support his teammate.
She expertly cleared out opponents from the ruck.

senses.definition: (rugby) a method of restarting play where players from both teams bind together and push to contest for the ball

examples.x: The teams engaged in a scrum to contest possession.
He played as a prop in the scrum formation.
The scrum collapsed, and the referee reset it.
They won the scrum and quickly passed the ball out.
Their scrum dominated the opposition.

senses.definition: a fast throw-in taken by a rugby player from the sideline to restart play quickly

examples.x: He made a quick-throw to catch the defense off guard.
The referee signaled for a quick-throw to be taken.
He launched the ball with a quick-throw into the field of play.
She sprinted to take the quick-throw before the defense could set.
The team opted for a quick-throw instead of a lineout.

senses.definition: the act of seizing or catching a pass intended for another player in rugby

examples.x: The winger made a crucial interception near the try line.
The scrum-half's interception led to a breakaway try.
His interception halted the opposition's promising attack.
The fullback's interception turned the tide in their favor.
She scored from an interception just before halftime.

senses.definition: (rugby) a kick at goal taken to score points after a try or penalty

examples.x: He lined up the goal kick with precision.
She missed the goal kick by a few inches.
The game was decided by a crucial goal kick.
The team celebrated after a successful goal kick.
The crowd went silent during the goal kick.

senses.definition: (in rugby) to pass the ball back in a scrum or to move it along the ground

examples.x: The scrum-half hooked the ball cleanly out of the scrum and passed it swiftly to the fly-half.
The player skillfully hooked the ball with his foot and directed it towards the waiting forward.
Facing intense pressure from the opposing pack, the hooker struggled to hook the ball cleanly.
The scrum-half's quick reflexes allowed him to hook the ball before the opposing team could gain possession.
Despite the muddy conditions, the hooker was able to hook the ball cleanly and distribute it to his teammates.
